title: Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
text:
Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB) - SupportGoWhere
Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB)
Share link
Provides up to 4 weeks of income support, for eligible working fathers who do not qualify for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL).
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
For children born or adopted before 1 Jan 2024, eligible fathers can receive up to 2 weeks of Government-Paid Paternity Benefit (GPPB) , capped at $2,500 per week.
For children born or adopted on or after 1 Jan 2024, eligible fathers can receive up to 4 weeks of GPPB, capped at $2,500 per week.
For natural or adoptive fathers who do not qualify for GPPL due to their employment arrangement
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to 2 weeks of income support for eligible fathers for child born/estimated delivery/FIA date before January 1, 2024, capped at $2,500 per week.

Up to 4 weeks of income support for eligible fathers for child born/estimated delivery date/FIA on or after January 1, 2024, capped at $2,500 per week.

Who is this for?

For natural fathers with live births:

Your child’s date of birth/estimated delivery date is on or after 1 Jan 2021.

Your child is a Singapore Citizen or becomes one within 12 months of birth

For natural fathers with stillbirths:

The date of stillbirth is on or after November 1, 2021

Either you or the child’s mother is a Singapore citizen

For adoptive fathers:

You are not the natural father of the child

Your FIA date is on or after 1 Jan 2021

The child must be below 12 months old on FIA date

If child is not a Singapore citizen, a dependent’s pass (DP) has been issued and either you or your wife are Singapore citizens.

Applicable to all:

Employed or self-employed for at least 90 days in the 12 months before child's birth

Lawfully married to the child's mother between conception and birth or within 12 months of birth

Not eligible for Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL) except under specific conditions such as end of employment contract or retrenchment

How to apply?

Record employment or self-employment details for the 12 months before your child's birth or Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date.

Submit your claim online here within 15 months of your child's birth or FIA date.

An officer will contact you following your submission.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222 
(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
